Title: Display successful batch run statistics
Description: Verify that a completed batch execution shows a successful status and relevant metadata.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a batch run that completed successfully.
3. Review the details shown for that run.
Expected Results: The batch appears with a successful status and includes run timestamp, batch identifier, and completion details.

Title: Flag failed batch runs
Description: Ensure failed batch executions are clearly highlighted for review.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a batch run that failed.
3. Review the displayed status and details.
Expected Results: The failed batch is displayed with an error or failed status and is visibly flagged for investigation.

Title: Handle incomplete batch metadata
Description: Verify that missing or incomplete batch run data is represented safely instead of being mistaken for success.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a batch record with missing execution information.
3. Review the status shown for that record.
Expected Results: The system displays the batch as incomplete or unknown and does not misrepresent it as successful.

Title: Display successful job run details
Description: Verify that completed jobs are shown with their execution status and identifying information.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Navigate to the job run statistics section.
3. Select a completed job entry.
Expected Results: The job appears as successful and includes the job identifier and execution details.

Title: Show failed job outcomes
Description: Ensure failed job executions are visible with relevant error context.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a job record that failed.
3. Review the status and supporting details.
Expected Results: The failed job is clearly marked as failed and includes available error context for investigation.

Title: Represent in-progress or skipped jobs
Description: Verify that jobs that are still running or skipped are shown clearly and not omitted.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a job that is in progress or skipped.
3. Review the displayed status.
Expected Results: The system displays the correct in-progress or skipped state and provides enough context to interpret the job outcome.

Title: Correlate job and batch results
Description: Verify that job outcomes can be correlated to the parent batch result for end-to-end monitoring.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a specific batch and its associated jobs.
3. Compare the job statuses with the batch summary.
Expected Results: The user can see how individual job outcomes contribute to the overall batch status.

Title: Display successfully processed files
Description: Verify that successfully processed files are listed with a success status.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Navigate to the file run statistics section.
3. Select a file that has completed successfully.
Expected Results: The file is shown with a successful processing status and the associated file reference.

Title: Flag files with processing issues
Description: Ensure files that encountered issues are clearly indicated.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a file with a processing problem.
3. Review the status and file details.
Expected Results: The file is marked as having an issue and the problem is visible to the user.

Title: Handle empty or duplicate file entries
Description: Verify that incomplete or duplicate file records are treated explicitly rather than appearing as successful.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Select a file record that is empty, duplicate, or partially populated.
3. Review how it is presented.
Expected Results: The system shows the file record as incomplete or duplicate and does not misrepresent it as successful.

Title: Cross-check file status with workflow context
Description: Verify that file-level results can be interpreted in the broader workflow context.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow monitoring view.
2. Review a file entry and its related batch/job context.
3. Compare the file result with the parent workflow result.
Expected Results: The user can understand how file outcomes relate to the overall workflow status.

Title: View consolidated workflow summary
Description: Verify that the consolidated reporting view presents batch, job, and file outcomes together.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow summary view.
2. Review the batch, job, and file sections.
3. Compare the displayed statuses.
Expected Results: The summary view shows a unified representation of workflow health across all three levels.

Title: Highlight workflow exceptions
Description: Ensure that problematic areas in the overall workflow summary are clearly surfaced.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow summary view.
2. Select a summary containing failed batch, job, or file items.
3. Review the highlighted results.
Expected Results: The summary view clearly identifies the areas where the workflow has exceptions or failures.

Title: Handle partial workflow data
Description: Verify that a summary with incomplete data is shown appropriately without implying full success.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow summary view.
2. Review a summary where one or more monitoring components have missing data.
3. Assess the displayed state.
Expected Results: The summary clearly indicates incomplete or partial data rather than implying a fully successful workflow.

Title: Drill down from summary to detailed results
Description: Verify that the consolidated summary allows navigation to detailed batch, job, and file information.
Steps:
1. Open the workflow summary view.
2. Select a summary item that requires deeper inspection.
3. Navigate to the detailed view for that item.
Expected Results: The user can drill into underlying details from the summary view and understand the root cause or status of the selected component.
